attributes.name: DLL Side-Loading - VC++ Runtime from Non-Standard Path
attributes.description: Detects loading of Visual C++ runtime DLLs (msvcp140.dll, vcruntime140.dll) from directories outside of Windows, Program Files, or Program Files (x86). APT33 Tickler uses DLL sideloading by placing malicious versions of these DLLs alongside a renamed Microsoft binary. Any tool performing this technique will trigger this detection.
attributes.query: library where dll.name : ("msvcp140.dll", "vcruntime140.dll", "msvcp140d.dll", "vcruntime140d.dll") and not dll.path : ("C:\\Windows\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\*") and not process.executable : ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\Windows\\*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Portable applications bundling VC++ redistributable DLLs
Game launchers with bundled runtime libraries

attributes.name: Renamed Microsoft Binary with SharePoint Theme Execution
attributes.description: Detects execution of binaries named as Microsoft SharePoint components (Microsoft.SharePoint.NativeMessaging.exe, SharePoint.exe, etc.) from directories outside of Program Files. APT33 Tickler renames legitimate Microsoft binaries to blend with SharePoint components. The detection checks for version info mismatches where the original filename does not match the executing filename.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("Microsoft.SharePoint.NativeMessaging.exe", "SharePoint.exe", "Microsoft.SharePoint.exe", "SharePointSync.exe") and not process.executable : ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\Windows\\*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: ClickOnce-deployed SharePoint add-ins running from AppData
Legitimate SharePoint migration tools in non-standard paths

attributes.name: Registry Run Key Persistence to Non-Standard Path
attributes.description: Detects creation or modification of registry Run key values that point to executables outside of standard installation directories (Program Files, Windows). APT33 Tickler creates a Run key named 'SharePoint' pointing to a sideloaded binary in a user-writable directory. This detection is generic enough to catch any attacker abusing Run keys with non-standard paths.
attributes.query: registry where event.type == "change" and registry.path : ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*", "*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") and registry.data.strings : "*Users*" and not registry.data.strings : ("*Program Files*", "*Windows\\System32*")

technique.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate user-installed applications adding Run keys

attributes.name: SharePoint-Themed Registry Run Key Persistence
attributes.description: Detects registry Run key additions specifically using SharePoint-related value names or referencing SharePoint-named executables. This is a high-confidence indicator of APT33 Tickler persistence where the backdoor registers as 'SharePoint' in the Run key to blend with legitimate Microsoft software.
attributes.query: registry where event.type == "change" and registry.path : ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\SharePoint", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\SharePoint") and not registry.data.strings : ("*Program Files*", "*Program Files (x86)*")

technique.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Very unlikely - legitimate SharePoint components use Program Files paths

attributes.name: Scheduled Task Creation with SharePoint-Themed Binary
attributes.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks that reference SharePoint-themed executables or use SharePoint-related task names. APT33 Tickler creates a task named 'MicrosoftSharePointSync' with ONLOGON trigger pointing to the sideloaded binary as redundant persistence alongside the registry Run key.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "schtasks.exe" and process.args : "/Create" and process.args : ("*SharePoint*", "*NativeMessaging*", "*MicrosoftSharePointSync*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Legitimate SharePoint sync utilities creating scheduled tasks

attributes.name: Elevated Scheduled Task with Logon Trigger to Non-Standard Path
attributes.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks with ONLOGON or ONSTART triggers and HIGHEST run level that reference executables outside standard installation directories. This broader detection catches persistence mechanisms regardless of naming convention, covering APT33 Tickler and similar threat actors.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : "schtasks.exe" and process.args : "/Create" and process.args : ("ONLOGON", "ONSTART") and process.args : ("HIGHEST", "SYSTEM") and not process.args : ("*Program Files*", "*Windows\\System32*")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: System management tools creating elevated logon tasks
Software updaters with elevated scheduled tasks

attributes.name: Outbound Connection on APT33 Tickler C2 Ports 808 and 880
attributes.description: Detects outbound network connections to TCP ports 808 and 880, which are the documented command-and-control ports used by APT33 Tickler backdoor communicating with Azure-hosted infrastructure. These are non-standard HTTP ports that legitimate software rarely uses for outbound connections.
attributes.query: network where event.type == "start" and destination.port in (808, 880) and not process.name : ("svchost.exe", "services.exe", "System") and not destination.ip : ("127.0.0.1", "::1")
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Internal web applications on non-standard ports

attributes.name: Archive File with Double Extension Masquerading as Document
attributes.description: Detects creation of archive files (ZIP, RAR, 7z) with double extensions that masquerade as documents (e.g., .pdf.zip, .doc.zip). APT33 Tickler is delivered as 'Q3_Financial_Report_2025.pdf.zip' to trick users into opening what appears to be a PDF document. This delivery technique is used by multiple threat actors for spearphishing.
attributes.query: file where event.type == "creation" and file.name regex~ """.*\.(pdf|doc|docx|xls|xlsx|ppt|pptx|rtf)\.(zip|rar|7z|tar\.gz)$"""
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
false_positives: Backup scripts creating compressed document collections
